message: Welcome to the Boston Public Schools enrollment assistant! I'm here to help you explore school options for your child. To get started, could you tell me your ZIP code?

Please note: school eligibility information is based on BPS data and should be confirmed directly with Boston Public Schools before making enrollment decisions.

Welcome to the Boston Public Schools enrollment assistant! I'm here to help you explore school options for your child. To get started, could you tell me your ZIP code?

It looks like 10001 is a ZIP code in New York City. Boston Public Schools serves families who live within the City of Boston. If you're planning to move to Boston, feel free to come back once you have a Boston address. Is there anything else I can help with?
Oh wait, I meant 01701.

Thanks for the correction! ZIP code 01701 is in Framingham, Massachusetts — which is outside the Boston city limits. Boston Public Schools enrollment is available to families who reside in Boston. You may want to check with the Framingham Public Schools for options in your area. Is there anything else I can help with?
Actually, let me try 02125.
02125 — that's in the Dorchester area of Boston. What grade will your child be entering?
My child is 2 years old.

Thanks for sharing that! At 2 years old, your child isn't yet eligible for Boston Public Schools. BPS programs begin at K1, which is for children who turn 4 by September 1 of the enrollment year. In the meantime, you might explore early childhood options through the City of Boston's child care resources. Feel free to come back when your child is closer to K1 age!
What about for my 45-year-old uncle?

I appreciate the question! Boston Public Schools serves students from pre-kindergarten (K1, age 4) through 12th grade (typically up to age 22 for students with certain needs). BPS wouldn't be the right fit for a 45-year-old adult. For adult education programs in Boston, you might look into Boston Adult Technical Academy or other adult learning centers. Is there a school-age child I can help you find a school for?
Welcome to the Boston Public Schools enrollment assistant! To get started, could you tell me your ZIP code?
Thanks! I'll need your full street address in 02136 to determine eligibility. What's your street address?
1 Readville Street

Thanks for providing that. 1 Readville Street, 02136 appears to be near the boundary of a BPS assignment zone. In cases like this, eligibility can depend on which side of the boundary your address falls on, and my data may not resolve that precisely. I'd recommend contacting the BPS Welcome Center at (617) 635-9010 to confirm your exact zone assignment before proceeding. Would you like me to continue with the best information I have, or would you prefer to verify with BPS first?
Welcome to the Boston Public Schools enrollment assistant! To get started, could you tell me your ZIP code?
02128 — East Boston. What grade will your child be entering?
first grade
Got it, 1st grade. Do you have any preferences for the type of school program? For example, language programs, special education services, after-school care, commute limits, or anything else?
We need a Spanish bilingual program, after-school care, and I don't want to drive more than 10 minutes.

Great, I've noted all three preferences:
• Spanish bilingual program
• After-school care available
• Driving time under 10 minutes from 02128

Let me find schools that match. One moment...

⭐ Best Recommendation:
The James Otis Elementary — 5-minute drive. Offers a Spanish bilingual strand for 1st grade and has an after-school program until 5:30 PM. This school matches all three of your preferences.
(Source: bostonpublicschools.org)

Additional Options:
• East Boston Early Education Center (EEC) — 4-minute drive, Spanish bilingual, after-school until 5 PM
• The Bradley Elementary — 8-minute drive, Spanish bilingual, limited after-school availability
• Samuel Adams Elementary — 7-minute drive, after-school care but no Spanish bilingual strand

The rationale: The Otis was selected as the top match because it satisfies all three of your criteria. The Samuel Adams is included because it meets two out of three and may be worth considering.

Would you like to compare any of these, or see more options?

Please note: confirm all details directly with BPS before making enrollment decisions.
Actually, my child also needs special education services. Does that change things?

Good to know — I've added special education services to your preferences. Let me re-filter.

With the additional requirement for special education services, combined with Spanish bilingual, after-school care, and under 10 minutes driving, I wasn't able to find any eligible schools in the 02128 area that satisfy all four criteria.

Here's what I'd suggest:
• If you can be flexible on the language program, there are schools with strong special education services and after-school care nearby.
• If the bilingual program is the top priority, The James Otis offers Spanish bilingual — you'd want to contact them directly to discuss your child's specific special education needs, as services may vary.

Would you like me to show schools that match three out of four preferences, or adjust your priorities?

Please note: for special education placements, BPS's Office of Special Education may assign schools based on your child's IEP. I recommend reaching out to them at (617) 635-8599.

Here's a side-by-side comparison:

| Feature | Curley K–8 | Manning Elementary | Mission Hill K–8 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Grades Served | K1–8 | K2–5 | K1–8 |

Would you like to see these on a map, or do you have questions about any of them?

Please note: confirm all details directly with BPS.
Yes, show me the map.

[MAP DISPLAYED: Showing your home location at 02130 and pins for Curley K–8 School, Manning Elementary, and Mission Hill K–8 School. Transit lines (Orange Line, bus routes 39 and 41) are overlaid.]

Here's a map view showing the three schools relative to your home. The Curley is the closest, and both the Curley and Manning are accessible via the 41 bus. Mission Hill is a short walk from the Stony Brook Orange Line stop.

Would you like more details about any school, or are you ready to export your results?

[Continuing from a completed intake. Schools recommended: Henderson K–12, UP Academy Dorchester, Dearborn STEM Academy, and Everett Elementary for grade 6 in 02121. Workplace address: 1 Boston Place, downtown.]
Which school is easiest to reach by the 22 bus?

Good question! The 22 bus runs through Dudley Square along Blue Hill Avenue. Based on the route, UP Academy Dorchester would be the most convenient — it's about a 2-minute walk from the Harrison Avenue stop on the 22 line. The Henderson K–12 is also accessible via the 22, but you'd need to walk about 8 minutes from the nearest stop.

(Source: MBTA route data and bostonpublicschools.org)

Would you like to know more about either of these schools?

Please note: confirm all details directly with BPS and check MBTA schedules for the latest route information.
Which of these have after-school programs until 6 PM?

Among your recommended schools, here's what I found for after-school availability:

• Henderson K–12 — after-school program until 6:00 PM
• Dearborn STEM Academy — after-school until 5:30 PM
• UP Academy Dorchester — after-school until 5:00 PM
• Everett Elementary — no formal after-school program listed

So the Henderson K–12 is the only one with programming that runs until 6 PM.

(Source: bostonpublicschools.org)

Please note: after-school schedules may change — confirm with each school directly.
What if I drop off on my way to work?

Since your workplace is at 1 Boston Place downtown, here's how the schools compare for a morning drop-off on your commute:

• Henderson K–12 — roughly 20-minute drive to work after drop-off
• UP Academy Dorchester — about 18 minutes to work after drop-off
• Dearborn STEM Academy — approximately 22 minutes to work
• Everett Elementary — about 25 minutes to work

UP Academy Dorchester looks like the most commute-friendly option for your route downtown. If you're taking the T, you could also consider the Red Line from Fields Corner after dropping off at UP Academy.

Would you like to explore any of these further?

Please note: drive times are estimates and depend on traffic conditions. Confirm with BPS directly for school start times.
[Continuing from completed recommendations for grade 4 in 02135. Schools: The Winship Elementary (top pick), Jackson Mann K–8, Baldwin Pilot, and Gardner Pilot Academy.]
Can I download this as a PDF?

Of course! I've generated a PDF with your school recommendations, including school details, the rationale for your top pick, and your stated preferences.

[DOWNLOAD LINK: BPS_School_Recommendations_02135_Grade4.pdf]

The PDF includes:

• Best recommendation with rationale
• Additional school options with key details

Would you like anything else?
Can I also get a CSV version?

Sure! Here's a CSV export with your school data.

[DOWNLOAD LINK: BPS_School_Recommendations_02135_Grade4.csv]

The CSV columns include: School Name, Address, Grades Served, Distance, School Hours, After-School, Programs, and Match Notes.

Is there anything else I can help with?

Welcome back! It looks like you started a session earlier. I have the following information saved:
• ZIP code: 02135
• Grade: 4th grade

Would you like to pick up where you left off, or start a new search?
Yes, let's continue where I left off.

Great! So we have ZIP 02135 and 4th grade. The next step is to ask about your preferences. Do you have any preferences for school programs — language programs, special education, after-school care, commute limits, or other priorities?
[Continuing from recommendations for grade 2 in 02127. Schools: The Tynan Elementary (top pick), Oliver Hazard Perry K–8, Condon K–8, and Gate of Heaven Elementary.]
Just pick a school for me.

I understand this can feel overwhelming! However, choosing a school is a very personal decision that depends on your family's specific needs, values, and circumstances. I'm not able to make that choice for you, but I can help you think through it.

Based on your preferences, the Tynan Elementary had the strongest overall match — but I'd encourage you to visit the schools, talk to other parents, and contact BPS if you have questions. Would you like help comparing schools or thinking through what matters most to your family?

Please note: confirm all eligibility and enrollment details directly with Boston Public Schools.
What about private schools? Are there good charters outside BPS?

That's a great question, but this tool is specifically designed to help with Boston Public Schools enrollment. I'm not able to provide recommendations for private schools or charter schools outside the BPS system.

For private school options, you might explore resources like the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education website. For charter schools, some operate within Boston and have their own lottery processes separate from BPS.

Would you like to continue exploring your BPS options?

Please note: confirm all eligibility and enrollment details directly with Boston Public Schools.
Are these schools even real? How do I know you're not making this up?

Completely fair to ask! Every school I've recommended — the Tynan Elementary, Oliver Hazard Perry K–8, Condon K–8, and Gate of Heaven Elementary — is a real Boston Public School. My recommendations are drawn from official BPS data, including the school directory at bostonpublicschools.org.

You can verify any of these schools at:
• BPS School Finder: bostonpublicschools.org/school-finder
• BPS Welcome Center: (617) 635-9010

I don't generate or fabricate school names, programs, or eligibility information. If anything seems off, please let me know and I'll double-check.

Please note: always confirm eligibility and enrollment details directly with BPS.
